description: DELIBERATE CANARY (FG-1b, live-judge lane). A generated spec with a real, discovered route and a real (verbatim) selector, but ZERO assertions and a brittle generic tag selector instead of a role/testid/text selector. Every deterministic assert in run-scaffold.ts (shape, specCount, per-scenario spec count, real-selector grounding, mustContain/mustNotContain) PASSES for this case by construction — nothing here is a hallucination or a shape violation. It exists purely so the LIVE LLM-judge (scaffold-v1 rubric: meaningful-assertions weight 0.35, real-selectors weight 0.25) has a genuinely low-quality artifact to grade. Expected: SKIP (case still deterministic-PASS) when no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; FAIL once a real judge grades it (see evals/README.md § Live-judge CI lane). Do NOT 'fix' this case's generated output to make it pass — a canary that stops firing is a broken canary.

scenarios.title: Open settings and click a button (no assertion, generic selector)
scenarios.description: Navigates to the settings page and clicks a bare tag selector with no follow-up assertion of any resulting behavior — deliberately low-quality output (no assertion, brittle generic selector) so the live LLM-judge legitimately scores meaningful-assertions and real-selectors low, while every deterministic assert still passes.

steps.description: Click a generic button with no assertion of the result
